steps.title: Find the graph equation
steps.content: From the graph, the line passes through $(-2,-3)$ and $(0,9)$. The slope is $$\frac{9-(-3)}{0-(-2)}=\frac{12}{2}=6$$ so the line is $$y=6x+9$$

steps.title: Match the answer form
steps.content: Rewrite the equation to make the constant $18$: $$2y=12x+18$$ $$-12x+2y=18$$ This matches $-12x+Ry=18$ with positive constant $R=2$. The answer is D.
